checklist.question: Did receivables grow materially faster than revenue across consecutive periods?

evidence.location: 10-Q FY2018 Q1 balance sheet / income statement footnote for FY

evidence.quote: Receivables-to-revenue ratio rose from ~6.55% (FY2009: ReceivablesNetCurrent=922,166,274.20; SalesRevenueGoodsNet=14,078,946,773.71) to ~10.70% (FY2018) over the observed period, with the sharpest jump in the final two years (9.16%→10.70%) coinciding with a large acquisition

checklist.question: Did non-current 'soft' assets (intangibles, other assets, capitalized items) grow materially faster than the business overall?

evidence.location: 10-Q balance sheet and annual revenue comparison

checklist.question: Is there a persistent divergence between net income and operating cash flow?

evidence.quote: NetIncomeLoss=2,061,187,676.03 (FY2017-05-29 to 2018-05-27) vs NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivities=2,747,927,821.49 (same period) - OCF exceeds NI
NetIncomeLoss=1,181,289,774.16 (FY2014-05-26 to 2015-05-31) vs NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivities=2,459,496,960.40 (same period) - OCF exceeds NI even in the weakest earnings year

checklist.question: Do inventory levels or gross margins show trends inconsistent with revenue?

evidence.quote: InventoryNet=1,434,996,732.13 (2017-05-28) vs InventoryNet=1,588,400,939.27 (2018-05-27), +10.7% growth, while SalesRevenueGoodsNet grew only ~0.8% over the same fiscal year (15,108,089,752.26 to 15,224,738,853.03)

Gross margin computed from GrossProfit/SalesRevenueGoodsNet ranged narrowly between ~33.75% (FY2015) and ~35.62% (FY2017), showing no severe divergence from revenue trend outside the acquisition-affected FY2018 (~34.48%)
evidence.location: 10-K quarterly GrossProfit and annual SalesRevenueGoodsNet series

checklist.question: Is the earnings trajectory unusually smooth relative to the volatility of revenue and cash flow?

evidence.quote: NetIncomeLoss annual fell from 1,764,632,001.95 (FY2014) to 1,181,289,774.16 (FY2015), a ~33% decline, then recovered to 1,641,792,567.48 (FY2016) and 1,603,199,705.78 (FY2017)

checklist.question: Did liability, allowance, or reserve balances decline (or fail to grow) despite growing business activity?

evidence.quote: AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent=23,503,923.29 (2017-05-28) against ReceivablesNetCurrent=1,383,249,411.31 (2017-05-28), a ratio of ~1.70% - the allowance ratio declined even as receivables grew

checklist.question: Does the filing chronology show irregularities (late filings/NT forms, amendments, unusual 8-K frequency)?

evidence.quote: No NT 10-K/NT 10-Q filings appear in the chronology near the cutoff date; the most recent 10-K/A or 10-Q/A amendments occurred in 2007-01-08, well before the analysis window, and regular quarterly/annual filings (10-Q accession 0001193125-18-276507 filed 2018-09-18) continued on a normal cadence

checklist.question: Is the provided data sufficient in coverage and length for the above assessments?

evidence.quote: Data spans fiscal years 2008 through 2018 with quarterly granularity across Revenue (SalesRevenueGoodsNet), Receivables, Inventory, Goodwill, Intangibles, Cash Flow, Equity, and Liabilities concepts, sufficient to assess multi-year trends

top_signals: Multi-year uptrend in receivables-to-revenue ratio (from ~6.6% in FY2009 to ~10.7% in FY2018), with the sharpest acceleration in FY2017-FY2018 coinciding with a large acquisition

Goodwill (+61%) and other intangibles (+64%) grew far faster than revenue (+0.8%) in FY2018, driven by a major debt- and equity-funded acquisition (total assets +40%, long-term debt +66%, financing inflows of $5.27B), which elevates purchase-accounting and future impairment estimation risk
Allowance for doubtful accounts as a percentage of receivables declined from ~2.18% (FY2016) to ~1.70% (FY2017-FY2018) even as receivables balances grew, suggesting reduced reserve conservatism

No evidence of smoothed earnings, no persistent NI/OCF divergence, and no late-filing or restatement irregularities near the cutoff date, which reduces overall concern despite the acquisition-related balance sheet volatility
